canonical_question: What are SaaS gross margin benchmarks by delivery model (pure cloud, hybrid, managed service)?
aliases: SaaS gross margin by delivery model
Pure cloud vs hybrid SaaS gross margin
Software gross margin by company stage

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: 2025-2026 AI inference costs creating new margin tier between traditional SaaS and managed services

constraints: Benchmarks vary dramatically by delivery model — pure cloud (80-85%), hybrid with services (65-75%), managed service (40-60%) — using a single 'SaaS margin' number is meaningless
Company stage compresses margins — pre-$1M ARR companies run 40-60% even with pure cloud models due to over-provisioned infrastructure and high-touch support
COGS classification inconsistency across companies makes direct comparison unreliable — some bury support costs in opex, others in COGS
Vertical SaaS with embedded payments or hardware runs structurally lower margins (40-70%) that are healthy for their model
AI-native SaaS creates a new margin tier (25-60%) that fits between managed services and traditional SaaS — existing benchmarks do not apply

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User wants general SaaS margin targets and AI impact, not delivery model segmentation
User wants to understand how margins affect valuations and multiples
User wants total efficiency metric combining growth and profitability

often_confused_with.label: Bessemer Efficiency Score (growth + FCF margin combined, not gross margin alone)

inputs_needed.question: What is the company's delivery model?
options: Hybrid model with hardware or payments component
